title: Garlicky Croatian Flatbread Dough
description: A garlicky, pillowy, lazy-Sunday Croatian dish featuring self-raising flour, mantecare-emulsify, and FODMAP-friendly ingredients, perfect for a relaxing afternoon snack.

ingredients.name: self-raising flour
ingredients.notes: make sure to use FODMAP-friendly flour

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the self-raising flour, sugar, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the olive oil, minced garlic, and warm water.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a round flatbread.
Place the flatbread on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the flatbread from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
